title: grindelia stricta var. lanata
intro: Grindelia stricta var. lanata, also known as sticky gumweed, is a perennial herb that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the western United States and Canada. It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant is rich in flavonoids, terpenoids, and other bioactive compounds that provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Grindelia stricta var. lanata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal purposes, and it is also used topically as a compress or salve to promote healing and soothe skin conditions.
